description: Expected TypeScript semantic errors from NRT covariance mismatches in BCL

expectedErrors.reason: BCL has nullable return type (GetProperties returns PropertyDescriptorCollection?) but base CollectionConverter has non-nullable
BCL method signature mismatch - Get overloads differ between base and derived
BCL has nullable return (GetParameters returns ParameterInfo[]?) but base MethodInfo has non-nullable
BCL nullable parameter mismatch in CreateKeyExchange overloads
BCL has nullable CreateNavigator() return but base XmlLinkedNode has non-nullable
BCL has nullable CreateNavigator() return but base XmlLinkedNode has non-nullable
BCL has nullable CreateNavigator() return but base XmlLinkedNode has non-nullable
BCL has nullable CreateNavigator() return but base XmlLinkedNode has non-nullable
BCL has nullable CreateNavigator() return but base XmlLinkedNode has non-nullable
BCL has nullable CreateNavigator() return but base XmlLinkedNode has non-nullable

notes: These 10 TS2430 errors are BCL NRT annotation inconsistencies, not tsbindgen bugs.
The errors occur when derived types have MORE nullable return types than their base classes.
In C# this is valid at runtime, but TypeScript's structural typing rejects it.
1. CreateNavigator() - XmlLinkedNode returns non-nullable but 6 derived classes return nullable
Zero syntax errors (TS1xxx) is required - these must always be zero.
